intro.title: CV support that is practical, calm, and one-to-one.
intro.locationNoteSuffix: Exact venue details are shared on enquiry.
intro.supportingNote: You can ask about the session before attending, including on someone else's behalf.

atAGlanceSection.title: Know the shape of the session before you travel.
atAGlanceSection.summary: Timing, venue handling, who it suits, and what to bring are visible without making the route feel formal.
atAGlanceSection.audienceSummary: Young people can come for direct help, and parents, carers, or referrers can ask first if they need to check the fit.
atAGlanceSection.bringSummary: A draft CV, a blank document, screenshots, notes, or just the question you need help with.

expectationSection.title: Practical support focused on the next useful step.
expectationSection.summary: The session feels like guided progress, not a test.

items.title: Start from where you are
items.body: Bring a CV draft, an application, or nothing at all. The session begins with what you already have and what feels stuck.

items.title: Work on real wording and next steps
items.body: Support can cover structure, wording, applications, confidence for asking questions, and practical planning after the session.

items.title: Leave with something clearer
items.body: The aim is progress you can use straight away, such as a stronger CV, a clearer application, or a better sense of the next action.

supportSection.title: Keep anxiety low and the next step obvious.
supportSection.summary: Safety, privacy, and simple ways to ask first stay visible alongside the session detail.
supportSection.checklistTitle: Support and reassurance that stay visible

items.title: Ask before attending
items.body: If you want to check the session is right, contact is okay before travelling. This also works for parents, carers, and referrers.

items.title: Venue detail is handled carefully
items.body: Rochdale stays public, while exact location detail is shared privately on enquiry rather than published openly.

items.title: Safeguarding stays in view
items.body: The safeguarding and contact routes remain visible from this page so support questions never rely on hidden links.

note.title: Not sure if this is the right route?
note.body: Use the contact path if you want to check the session, ask on someone else's behalf, or explain a need briefly before anyone travels.

relatedProgrammeSection.title: This session sits inside the wider Career Support & CV Help route.
relatedProgrammeSection.summary: The programme page explains the broader support offer. This session page owns the exact Saturday timing, calendar file, and first-visit detail.

faqSection.title: Questions people often ask before a first visit.
faqSection.summary: These answers lower anxiety without pretending the session has confirmed details that are not yet public.

fallbackNotice.title: Need a different next step?
fallbackNotice.body: Use contact if you need help deciding whether CV support is the right route, or if the current Saturday time does not work.

ctaBand.title: Ask about the session or keep the wider career route in view.
ctaBand.summary: The fastest next step is to ask about CV support. If you are still deciding, the wider programme and contact routes stay available.

note.title: Safeguarding stays visible
note.body: If you need urgent help or need to raise a safeguarding concern, use the safeguarding route straight away.

intro.title: Youth club built around welcome, friendship, and safe space.
intro.locationNoteSuffix: Exact venue details are shared on enquiry.
intro.supportingNote: Come as you are. If a first visit feels like a big step, a young person or parent/carer can ask first.

atAGlanceSection.title: Make the first visit feel welcoming before anyone travels.
atAGlanceSection.summary: The route shows the weekly rhythm, how participation can look, and how venue detail is handled without turning the session into a formal intake process.
atAGlanceSection.audienceSummary: Young people are the main audience. Parents or carers can still ask about fit, atmosphere, and practical detail before a first visit if that helps.
atAGlanceSection.bringSummary: Nothing formal is required. Someone can come as they are, or ask first if having a clearer picture would make the visit feel easier.

expectationSection.title: A first visit should feel welcoming, social, and easy to grow into.
expectationSection.summary: The value here is atmosphere, repeated attendance, and connection over time rather than a one-off task.

items.title: Arrive without needing a perfect reason
items.body: Someone can come for company, activities, conversation, or simply to try the space.

items.title: More than one way to take part
items.body: Games, shared activities, and chill time mean participation does not have to look the same for everyone on the night.

items.title: Friendship builds through repetition
items.body: Because the session runs every week, familiar faces and confidence can grow over time instead of being forced in a single evening.

supportSection.title: Belonging should feel possible before the first visit.
supportSection.summary: Reassurance stays practical: ask-first contact, careful venue handling, and visible safeguarding rather than vague mood language.
supportSection.checklistTitle: Support and reassurance that stay visible

items.title: Ask before travelling
items.body: A young person can ask a question first, and parents or carers can check the fit without taking over the route.

items.title: Take the pressure off arrival
items.body: The public page makes room for someone who joins in straight away and someone who needs a little time to look around and settle.

items.title: Location and safeguarding stay clear
items.body: Rochdale is public, exact venue detail is handled on enquiry, and the safeguarding route stays visible if a concern needs a different response.

note.title: Want to ask about a first visit?
note.body: Use the contact route if a young person, parent, or carer wants to ask about the atmosphere, practical detail, or whether youth club feels like the right next step.

relatedProgrammeSection.title: This session sits inside the wider Community & Friendship route.
relatedProgrammeSection.summary: The programme page explains the broader belonging-first route across community and friendship. This session page owns the live Saturday timing, calendar file, and first-visit detail.

faqSection.title: Questions people often ask before a first visit.
faqSection.summary: These answers reduce first-visit anxiety, explain participation honestly, and keep unverified youth-work specifics out of the page.

fallbackNotice.title: Need a different next step?
fallbackNotice.body: Use contact if you want to ask about fit before attending, or if stepping back to the wider Community & Friendship route feels more useful first.

ctaBand.title: Ask about youth club or keep the wider belonging route in view.
ctaBand.summary: The clearest next step is to ask about the session. The wider programme and sessions hub stay visible if someone needs more context first.

note.title: Safeguarding stays visible
note.body: If there is an urgent concern or safeguarding issue, use the safeguarding route straight away.
